sanskrit: अनन्तश्चास्मि नागानां वरुणो यादसामहम् । पितृ़णामर्यमा चास्मि यमः संयमतामहम् ॥
hindiTranslation: नागोंमें अनन्त शेषनाग और जलजन्तुओंका अधिपति वरुण मैं हूँ ।  पितरोंमें अर्यमा और शासन करनेवालोंमें यमराज मैं हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: Among serpents I am Ananta; among water beings I am Varuna. Among ancestors I am Aryaman; among those who govern, I am Yama.
essence: The divine is visible in every order of existence.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says he is the highest presence among serpents, water beings, ancestors, and rulers. He is showing that his presence runs through every level of the world.
simpleInsight: Nothing is spiritually ordinary; even structures of power and lineage can be seen as sacred.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is overwhelmed and Krishna begins revealing how his presence appears throughout the world. Here he names serpents, water beings, ancestors, and rulers, showing that the same divine reality animates both nature and social order.
modernRelevance: You sit in a room full of titles, family history, and hidden hierarchies. This verse asks you to see the same sacred presence moving through all of it, not just in obvious holy moments.
translationLiteral: अनन्तः (Ananta) / च (and) / अस्मि (I am) / नागानाम् (of serpents) / वरुणः (Varuna) / यादसाम् (of water beings) / अहम् (I) / पितृणाम् (of ancestors) / अर्यमा (Aryaman) / च (and) / अस्मि (I am) / यमः (Yama) / संयमताम् (of those who govern) / अहम् (I am)
